prompt: A chimney-style range hood with a boxy lower canopy, a tall chimney stack, and two large rotary knobs above a row of three push buttons. The two knobs rotate continuously on their front shafts, and the three buttons move independently as small prismatic controls beneath them. Residential wall-mounted hood scale.

A four-burner gas stove top with a flat inset surface and five front rotary knobs aligned along the lower edge. The five knobs rotate continuously on their front-facing shafts below the burner field. Countertop cooktop scale.

A clamp-on task lamp with a desk-edge clamp, two spring-balanced arm links, and a small cylindrical shade. The lower arm rotates on a hinge above the clamp, the upper arm rotates on a hinge at the elbow, and the shade tilts on a hinge at the arm tip. Desk-edge lighting scale.

A writing desk with a rectangular top, four straight legs, and one shallow center drawer below the front edge. The drawer slides prismatically on straight internal runners beneath the top. Home office desk scale.

A stand mixer with a fixed head, a bowl-lift frame, and a side lever that raises the bowl under the whisk. The bowl platform moves vertically on the lift frame, the side lever rotates on a short pivot at the column, and the whisk spins continuously on the vertical drive axis. Countertop baking scale.

A workshop ceiling light fixture with a rectangular housing and a hinged lower diffuser panel. The diffuser panel rotates downward on a long hinge along one side of the housing. Garage ceiling-light scale.

A laboratory blender with a boxy housing, a removable cup, and a latch-down top clamp over the cup opening. The top clamp rotates downward on a rear hinge, the cup rotates on its front mounting seat, and the blade spins continuously within the cup chamber. Lab instrument scale.

A kitchen under-counter unit with a narrow rectangular cabinet body and three vertically stacked drawers of equal depth, each sliding on soft-close prismatic guide rails with integrated recessed bar pull handles on the drawer front faces.

A compact pancake lens with a shallow barrel, a narrow focus ring, and a small manual aperture ring. The focus ring rotates continuously on its coaxial band, and the aperture ring rotates continuously around the rear portion of the barrel. Small camera-lens scale.

A small desk box fan with a square housing, a U-shaped tilt stand, and a front corner control knob. The housing tilts on horizontal hinges at the ends of the U-stand, the fan blade spins continuously behind the grille, and the front corner knob rotates continuously on its short shaft. Desktop cooling scale.

A standard extension ladder with two parallel rail sections and a fly section that rises within the base rails. The fly section slides prismatically upward along the guide channels of the base section. Contractor ladder scale.

A tripod floor lamp with three legs, a central post, and a spotlight head in a side yoke near the top. The spotlight head tilts on a horizontal hinge between the yoke arms. Decorative room-light scale.

A clamp-on welding vise with a parallel C-clamp base that grips a workbench, a fixed rear jaw, and a prismatic sliding front jaw. The C-clamp base tightens on a revolute screw joint to clamp the bench. The front jaw slides prismatically on guide rods.

A woodworking drill press with a tall column, a rectangular tilting table, and a sliding fence stop block on the front rail. The table slides prismatically on the column, the table rotates on its horizontal tilt hinge, and the stop block slides prismatically along the fence rail. Woodshop drill scale.

A shutter remote with a compact body, a spring-loaded top cover, and two large front buttons. The top cover rotates upward on a short hinge at the rear edge, and the two front buttons depress independently as small prismatic controls. Camera accessory scale.

A flat wall-panel air purifier with a front access door and a rectangular filter cartridge behind it. The front access door rotates on a vertical hinge at the left edge, and the filter cartridge slides prismatically out on bottom guide rails behind the door. Wall-mounted purifier scale.

A display easel with a tripod base, a small top clamp that slides on the mast, and a lower support ledge. The tripod legs rotate on crown hinges, and the top clamp slides prismatically along the mast above the ledge. Lobby display scale.

A studio spotlight on a yoke stand with a round base, a vertical support, a U-shaped yoke, and a cylindrical lamp can. The yoke pans on a vertical joint at the stand top, and the lamp can tilts on a horizontal hinge between the yoke arms. Stage-light scale.
A pedestal swivel bar stool with a round base, a central post, and a circular seat. The seat rotates continuously about the vertical pedestal axis. Bar seating scale.

A digital thermostat with a shallow square body, a side rotary dial, and four front buttons. The side dial rotates continuously on its short shaft, and the four front buttons depress independently as small prismatic controls. Home wall-control scale.

A retro-style toaster oven with a wide body, two side-hinged front doors, and three chrome control knobs in a vertical stack. The two doors rotate outward on their vertical hinges at the body sides, and the three knobs rotate continuously on their front shafts. Countertop appliance scale.

A compact camera-like device mounted on a tripod with three folding legs, a pan head, and a tilt plate under the device body. The three legs rotate on hinges at the tripod crown, the head pans continuously about a vertical joint, and the device tilts on a horizontal hinge at the top plate. Tripod accessory scale.

An under-slung yaw-axis rotary module. A top support bracket carries the moving member below it, creating a different silhouette while keeping the same single-axis kinematics. One revolute joint turns the output face about the vertical support axis.
A parking barrier gate with a rectangular control housing and a counterweighted horizontal boom arm that lifts on a revolute hinge.
A bridge-guided linear carriage. A grounded bridge frame supports a single carried slider running through a boxed guide channel. The carried slider is one prismatic joint along the channel axis.

A fork-and-tab hinge assembly. A clevis-like fixed side captures a single moving tab between its cheeks with a through-pin and short barrel segments. The tab is one revolute joint about the supported pin axis.

A through-shaft rotary module. A fixed frame carries a straight shaft through a pair of side bearings with a compact output collar near one end. The shaft spins on a single continuous joint about its supported axis.

An under-slung roll-axis spindle module. A top support bracket carries the moving member below it, creating a different silhouette while keeping the same single-axis kinematics. One revolute joint spins the output flange about its own longitudinal shaft axis between fixed supports.
A DJ monitor wedge speaker on a tilt bracket. A rectangular speaker cabinet sits on a tilt base bracket. The speaker cabinet tilts on a revolute hinge at the bracket pivot about a horizontal axis.

An under-slung pitch-axis trunnion module. A top support bracket carries the moving member below it, creating a different silhouette while keeping the same single-axis kinematics. One revolute joint tilts the carried face about a horizontal trunnion axis supported by side cheeks.

A clicking slider fidget toy with a rectangular flat housing and a center thumb slider that slides back and forth. The thumb slider moves prismatically along the housing's long axis. A ratchet click mechanism engages at each end on a short revolute detent.

A stadium four-head floodlight mast with a round pole, a top cross-arm, and four individual lamp heads. The cross-arm is fixed to the pole top. Each lamp head tilts on its own revolute tilt joint at the arm mount bracket.

A front live-axle beam with a wide rectangular solid-steel beam, two kingpin steering knuckle housings each pivoting on vertical revolute kingpin joints at the beam ends, and two wheel hubs spinning on revolute axles at each knuckle face.

A three-joint robotic arm for pick-and-place work. A pedestal base carries a shoulder housing, upper arm link, forearm link, and compact wrist head. Joints: shoulder revolute about vertical, elbow revolute about horizontal, wrist revolute about the forearm axis. Offset the moving chain to one side of the fixed support.

A bridge-backed yaw-pitch module. A rear support frame carries a compact yaw base and a smaller pitch yoke with a simple output face. The yaw base and pitch yoke are revolute joints on vertical and horizontal axes.

A children's tabletop first telescope: a small refractor on a simple alt-az tripod. The telescope tube tilts on a revolute altitude joint on the tripod head. The tripod head rotates continuously on an azimuth bearing.

A robotic leg module for a walking machine. An upper leg housing carries a thigh link, shank link, and compact ankle-foot section. Joints: hip revolute about a lateral axis, knee revolute about a lateral axis, ankle revolute about a lateral axis. Offset the moving chain to one side of the fixed support.

A screw-cap bottle. A bottle body rises to a threaded neck, capped by a separate screw closure. Joints: the cap rotates continuously about the bottle neck axis. Make the rotating element narrow and long.

A screw-in light bulb seated in a socket. A bulb envelope sits above a threaded metal base, and the socket body holds the mating threaded collar. Joints: the bulb rotates continuously about the shared socket axis. Make the rotating element narrow and long.
A concrete bell tower with a plain rectangular shaft and a large bell on a steel frame yoke swinging on a revolute joint.

A city upright bike fork with a round steerer and two blade legs, and a wide swept-back handlebar on a tall stem. The tall stem clamps to the steerer on a revolute joint for angle. The swept-back bar rotates in the stem clamp on a revolute joint.

category_title: Clock tower with rotating hour and minute hands
prompt: A modern steel-and-glass clock tower with a square shaft and four large circular clock faces. Each clock face has a minute hand rotating continuously and an hour hand rotating continuously on the same central axis.

A narrow vestibule three-wing revolving door for a tight commercial entry. A slim central post carries three narrow rectangular panel wings. The wing assembly rotates continuously about the slim post axis.

category_title: Roof vent tower with hinged flap
prompt: A rooftop vent tower with a weather flap. An upright vent housing ends in a framed outlet, with one flap attached along the top edge. Joints: the flap is a single revolute panel about a horizontal top-edge axis. Keep the body shallow and compact around the opening.

category_title: Sewing box with simple hinged lid
prompt: A small sewing box with a plain top lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Keep the body shallow and compact around the opening.

A fixie track crankset with a solid right crank arm, a left arm, and a single keyed track chainring. The spindle and both crank arms rotate continuously. Each pedal rotates continuously about its axle.

A hospital stretcher elevator with a wide rectangular car and two wide sliding door panels. The car slides prismatically on vertical guide rail columns. Each door panel slides prismatically sideways on its guide rail track.

A hinged service access panel on an equipment face. A rectangular frame surrounds the opening, and a panel door hangs from one side with a latch edge opposite. Joints: the door is a single revolute panel about a vertical side hinge axis. Keep the body shallow and compact around the opening.

A single-leaf drawbridge. A fixed shore frame carries one bridge leaf on heavy side bearings. Joints: the bridge leaf is a single revolute panel about a horizontal shore-side axis. Keep the body shallow and compact around the opening.

A sliding security gate. A fixed post and frame support one gate leaf captured by a top track or bottom guide. Joints: the gate leaf translates prismatically along the track axis. Center the moving member on the main body axis.

A single-sash sliding window. A rectangular outer frame contains one fixed frame segment and one sliding sash. Joints: the sash translates prismatically along horizontal guide channels. Center the moving member on the main body axis.

category_title: CCTV mast with pan-tilt camera head
prompt: A telescoping CCTV mast with a two-stage nested pole on a ballast base and a box camera head on two revolute pan and tilt axes.

An outdoor wet-rated ceiling fan with a sealed motor housing on a downrod and two wide blade arms with blades. The blade assembly rotates continuously. The downrod mounts to the canopy on a fixed swivel joint.

A boxed dual-column lift carriage. Two grounded guide columns are tied by a base and top crosshead, and a rectangular carriage rides between them on wrapped guide blocks. One prismatic joint moves the carriage along the column axis.

A portal-lite dual-rail gantry. A grounded two-rail chassis carries a transverse beam riding on end trucks, and the beam supports a central runner. The beam translates prismatically along the base rails, and the runner translates prismatically along the beam axis.

A control cabinet with a tall narrow carcass, a single hinged outer door, and an inner hinged equipment sub-door. The outer door hinges on revolute knuckles at its left edge. The inner sub-door hinges on its own revolute knuckles inside the outer frame.

A edge-arm offset rotary stack. A grounded lower platform carries a stiff side arm, and the side arm supports a smaller upper rotary stage on a parallel offset axis. The lower platform and upper stage are separate revolute joints about parallel supported axes.
A stacked XY positioning stage. A grounded X base carries a crosswise Y carriage with a compact top table. The X and Y stages are orthogonal prismatic joints on supported guideways.

A searchlight tower with a pan-tilt lamp head. A mast support carries a yoke, and the lamp body sits between the yoke arms. Joints: the head pans on a vertical revolute base and tilts on a horizontal revolute yoke axis. Use one central support under the rotating stage.

A folding A-frame step ladder. A front climbing frame and a rear support frame meet at a top cap, with wide treads on the front frame. Joints: the rear frame folds relative to the front frame on a single revolute hinge at the top cap. Keep the folded envelope narrow and easy to store.

A skateboard with standard trucks. A deck supports front and rear truck hangers, each holding one axle and two wheels. Joints: each wheel spins continuously about its axle, and each truck steers on a revolute kingpin axis. Push the wheels farther outboard for a wider stance.

A stick vacuum with a folding wand. A motor body connects to a straight wand and a floor head, with the fold joint placed between the body and the wand. Joints: the fold joint is revolute about a horizontal axis, and the floor head pitches on a second revolute hinge. Offset the moving chain to one side of the fixed support.

A hand syringe. A cylindrical barrel holds a plunger rod and plunger head, ending in a nozzle tip. Joints: the plunger translates prismatically along the barrel axis. Center the moving member on the main body axis.

A two-stage telescoping slide. A grounded outer sleeve carries an intermediate member and an extending output member with a small end plate. The two stages slide on serial prismatic joints along the same axis.

Image-stabilized binoculars with two cylindrical objective barrels on a fixed bridge and an internal stabilizing prism block. The bridge hinge adjusts interpupillary distance on a revolute joint. An external focus wheel rotates continuously.

A stepped coaxial rotary stack. A grounded round base supports several concentric rotary stages on one common vertical shaft, with the stages clearly separated by diameter and height. The stages are independent revolute joints about the shared axis.

A compact mechanical keyboard. A rectangular case supports a grid of raised keycaps and a space bar row, with all keys arranged on a slightly recessed top plate. Each keycap is a small vertical prismatic slider with short travel normal to the case top.

category_title: Linear carriage with independent rotary end-effector
prompt: A linear carriage with an independent rotary end-effector. A grounded rail body carries one sliding carriage, and the carriage supports a short spindle or tool face on its own compact bearing housing. The carriage is a prismatic joint along the rail, and the carried spindle is a separate revolute joint about its own supported axis.

A mast-mounted fork carriage. A grounded mast with two guide rails carries a front carriage with a load backrest and paired forks projecting forward. The carriage is one vertical prismatic joint on the mast guides.

A box-section three-stage telescoping slide. A grounded outer section carries a middle runner, and the middle runner carries a smaller inner runner. The middle and inner runners are serial prismatic joints.

A three-arm turnstile gate. A central column supports a rotor hub with three radial arms inside a fixed frame. Joints: the arm hub rotates continuously about the vertical center axis. Use one central support under the rotating stage.

A two-joint prismatic chain. A grounded base guide carries a first carriage, and the first carriage carries a second linear stage aligned with or stepped from the first. The two moving stages are serial prismatic joints on their supported guide axes.
A bridge-backed vane array. A rear frame supports several parallel blades, each on its own pair of end pivots. The blades are independent revolute joints on parallel axes.

An XYZ cartesian stage. A grounded base carries a long X slide, the X carriage carries a crosswise Y slide, and the Y carriage carries a compact Z slide with a top tool plate. The X, Y, and Z axes are three stacked prismatic joints on orthogonal guide directions.

A hot-shoe camera flash with a rectangular mounting foot, a tall body, and a boxy flash head carried on a short articulated neck. The neck swivels on a vertical revolute joint above the body, and the flash head tilts on a horizontal revolute joint at the top of the neck. Small camera-accessory scale.

A coastal defense embrasure cannon with a wide rectangular gun platform on a fixed concrete barbette and a long smooth-bore barrel on an elevating pivot. The barrel elevates on a revolute trunnion joint at the platform carriage. The platform assembly traverses on a continuous horizontal revolute bearing on the concrete base.

A compact centrifugal juicer with a short cylindrical base, a clear upper lid, and a slim feed pusher guided through the center chute. Two small control dials sit side by side on the front of the base beneath the chamber. The feed pusher slides prismatically inside the chute, the lid rotates on a rear hinge, the two control dials rotate on their own short shafts, and the spinning basket rotates continuously about the vertical drive axis.

A commercial double-deck oven with two stacked rectangular baking chambers, each with its own front drop-down door. Each door drops on a revolute hinge at the door's bottom horizontal edge. Each chamber's hearth rack slides prismatically on side guide rails.

category_title: Desktop monitor with tilt swivel stand
prompt: A premium desktop monitor with a thin display shell, a broad stand column, and a heavy base plate, plus a rear cable-cover door on the stand spine. the stand head keeps enough depth for a real tilt and pivot assembly behind the screen, and the cable door is a real supported flap rather than a seam line. The screen housing tilts on a horizontal hinge at the stand head, rotates continuously about its central viewing axis for portrait mode, and the stand swivels continuously on a vertical axis at the base, and the cable-cover door rotates on a small hinge along one vertical edge. Professional desk-monitor scale.

A rack-mount 4U desktop tower chassis with a wide low-profile rectangular body, a hinged front bezel panel on a revolute hinge at the left edge, and a top access panel on two rear revolute hinge pins that lifts for internal access.

A boxed-body twin-slide gripper. A rigid center housing supports two mirrored jaw carriages with blunt vertical fingers and visible slide blocks at each side. The left and right jaw carriages are separate prismatic joints on opposed guideways.

A bench XZ transfer stage. A fixed base rail supports a horizontal carriage, and the carriage supports a compact upright mast with a moving vertical head. The horizontal carriage and vertical head are orthogonal prismatic joints.
A compact service YZ stage. A fixed back support carries a short lateral slide and a visibly smaller hanging Z carriage. The lateral and vertical stages are orthogonal prismatic joints.

A satellite communication dish on a pedestal azimuth-elevation mount. A parabolic reflector sits on a rear support frame with a feed arm reaching to the dish center, and the reflector frame is attached to a yoke on top of a rotating pedestal. The pedestal yaws on a vertical revolute axis, and the dish elevates on a horizontal revolute axis through the yoke sides.

A pan-base Z-slide module. A fixed lower rotary table carries an upright guide frame with a compact moving head. The rotary table is a revolute joint about vertical, and the head is a prismatic joint on the upright guide.

category_title: Tackle box with simple hinged lid
prompt: A tackle box with a plain hinged lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge, with internal tray details kept fixed inside the body. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Keep the body shallow and compact around the opening.

A desk fan with a tilting head. A base supports a guarded fan head between two side brackets. Joints: the fan rotor spins continuously about its axle, and the head tilts on a horizontal revolute axis through the side brackets. Use one central support under the rotating stage.

A record turntable. A plinth supports a central platter and a tonearm base off to one side. Joints: the platter rotates continuously about vertical, and the tonearm swings on a revolute pivot at the tonearm base. Use one central support under the rotating stage.

category_title: Watch winder box with hinged lid
prompt: A watch winder presentation box. A box body supports a top lid and one internal cradle mounted on a small spindle. Joints: the lid is revolute about a horizontal rear hinge axis, and the cradle rotates continuously about its spindle. Keep the body shallow and compact around the opening.

A bridge-backed three-axis wrist. A rear support carries a lower yaw stage, a smaller pitch frame, and a short roll spindle at the tip. The yaw, pitch, and roll functions are three serial revolute joints.

A benchtop monocular laboratory microscope with a horseshoe base, a curved arm, an inclined monocular tube, and a square mechanical stage. The objective nosepiece rotates about the vertical optical axis on a revolute turret joint, the stage carrier slides prismatically left to right on its cross-slide, and the full stage assembly slides prismatically fore and aft on the carrier support.

A small advertising blimp with an elongated envelope, a suspended gondola, tail fins, and two side propeller pods. The left and right propellers spin continuously on their shaft axes, and the rudder and elevator panels hinge from the trailing edges of the tail fins. Airship scale with a simple streamlined silhouette and a compact gondola.

A side-cheek dual finger assembly. A grounded palm plate supports two independent digit chains with stepped link sizes from root to tip. The left and right fingers are separate serial chains of three revolute joints each.

A rehab overbed table with an H-base on casters and a broad single top. Twin uprights carry a height-adjustable bridge under the tabletop, and the wide top is hinged along its rear edge. The bridge rises prismatically within the uprights, the tabletop rotates on a transverse hinge at the rear edge, and the casters spin and swivel on their joints.

A warehouse platform cart with a flat deck and four caster wheels. The low rectangular deck sits above corner caster mounts, and a push handle rises from one short end. Each caster wheel rotates on its axle, and each caster fork swivels on a vertical revolute pivot under the deck.

A compact PA speaker with a rigid top handle, a short rear kickstand, and a front control strip above the grille, plus three separate front buttons. the kickstand remains a visible supported part on the back shell, and the buttons sit as distinct parts in the control face. The rear kickstand rotates on a lower hinge and the main selector knob rotates continuously on the front panel, and the three buttons depress independently as small prismatic push controls. Portable rehearsal-speaker scale.

A compact remote weapon station on a low pedestal. A rotating base supports an elevation cradle with a boxy sensor unit on one side and a machine-gun-like barrel assembly on the other, all framed by armored side plates. The base yaws on a vertical revolute axis, and the weapon cradle pitches on a horizontal revolute axis between the side plates.

A molded rolling toolbox with a broad base, a recessed telescoping handle, and twin clear organizer covers on the lid. The handle slides prismatically upward in parallel channels, each organizer cover rotates on a small rear hinge, and the main transport wheels rotate continuously about the axle ends. Consumer workshop storage scale.

category_title: Standing desk with synchronous telescoping legs and articulated controls
prompt: A compact home-office standing desk with a narrow top, twin telescoping legs, and a small side control pod under one corner, plus a four-button keypad handset. the top frame remains visibly separate from the lifting columns, and the buttons are discrete parts in the handset face. The two leg stages slide prismatically within their outer columns as a synchronized pair, and the handset buttons articulate independently as small prismatic push controls. Small home-office desk scale.

A camera-mast style telescoping pan head. A fixed mast sleeve carries nested vertical stages, and the top stage carries a compact rotary plate. The mast stages are serial vertical prismatic joints, and the plate yaws on a revolute joint about the mast centerline.

A three-joint prismatic chain. A grounded base guide carries a first carriage, the first carriage carries a second linear stage, and the second stage carries a third stage at the tip. The three moving members are serial prismatic joints on their supported guide axes.

A traditional windmill with a rotating sail hub. A tower body supports a cap and a front hub carrying multiple lattice blades. Joints: the blade hub rotates continuously about the windmill shaft axis, and the cap rotates about the vertical tower axis. Use one central support under the rotating stage.

A vent-only skylight sunroof cassette with a small fixed glass panel and a sliding vent insert that opens toward the rear. The vent insert slides prismatically rearward on guide rails inside the panel frame.

A compact city kick scooter with a folding handlebar stem, a folding rear fender brake, and a folding deck hinge. The handlebar stem folds at the upper neck clamp on a revolute hinge. The deck folds at the center hinge on a revolute joint for compactness. Both wheels rotate continuously.

A flat home router with two fold-out paddle antennas on the back edge. The main body is a rounded rectangular slab with venting on top, and each antenna is a thin panel on a short pivot base. The two antennas rotate about horizontal root hinges from folded-flat to upright over about 100 degrees.

A two-tier pantry lazy Susan with a lower fixed base and one rotating shelf. The visible geometry is a broad circular tray above a low cylindrical support ring on a stable base plate. The tray spins continuously on a vertical revolute axis at the center.

A squat harbor lighthouse with a polygonal lantern room and exposed beacon mechanism. The upper lantern enclosure surrounds a central pedestal carrying a rotating light head, with a roof cap over the enclosure. The light head spins continuously on the central vertical revolute axis.

A rectangular louvered shutter panel. Two side stiles hold a stack of narrow slats, with each slat supported near its ends by pivot pins through the side rails. Every slat rotates on its own longitudinal revolute axis between the side supports.

A curbside mailbox with a rounded top and a front access door. The main box body is fixed to a short post or mounting plate, and the curved front door closes the mail opening with a small handle on its face. The front door rotates on a lower-edge revolute hinge axis by about 90 degrees.

A larger insulated pet flap with a thick outer frame and a clear central door panel. The flap is supported near its upper corners by pivot pins inside the frame hood. The flap rotates on a horizontal top revolute axis with about 90 degrees total swing.

A modern pickup tailgate with a stamped outer panel and an inner framed surface. The panel is attached between simplified bed-side supports and has a broad top cap and side latch points. A bottom-edge revolute hinge lets the tailgate fold from vertical closed to horizontal open.

A single-seat playground swing on an A-frame support. Two side frames and a top crossbeam support a belt seat suspended by two rigid hanger links. The seat assembly hangs from two top revolute joints at the crossbeam and swings in a vertical plane as a pendulum.

A routing-style portal gantry. Two grounded side frames support a bridge beam that travels on base rails, and a center carriage on the beam carries a vertical tool slide. The bridge, carriage, and tool head are prismatic joints along the base, beam, and vertical axes.

A slide-and-hinge chain. A grounded base guide carries a sliding carriage, and the carriage supports a hinged output link at its front face. The root joint is prismatic along the guide axis, and the carried output link rotates on a revolute pin axis.

A push-pull plunger chain. A grounded housing guides a straight plunger, and the plunger tip drives a short front lever captured on a transverse pin. The plunger is a prismatic joint along the housing axis, and the front lever is a revolute joint about the supported pin axis.

A radial arm with sliding carriage. A grounded base column supports a horizontal arm that sweeps in plan, and a compact carriage rides along the arm to a small head plate. The arm rotates about a vertical revolute joint at the column, and the carriage slides on a prismatic joint along the arm.

A box-shoulder elbow arm. A grounded root block carries a deep upper link and a slimmer forelink ending in a compact faceplate. The upper link and forelink are connected by serial shoulder and elbow revolute joints.

A box-tube telescoping reach boom. A fixed base bracket supports several nested rectangular boom members that step down in section size toward the tip. The boom members are serial prismatic joints along the shared boom axis.
A three-joint serial hinge chain. A base link supports two intermediate links and a short end link through offset hinge blocks. All three joints are revolute about parallel axes in one plane.

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: A compact two-branch inspection tree. A boxed spine supports two side-mounted rotary branches on separate brackets, with the branch lengths and mounting heights intentionally varied so the silhouette does not collapse into symmetry. The two branches are independent revolute joints on their own supported axes.

A side-post cantilever arm. A grounded post supports a shoulder link, a forelink, and a short wrist face, with the whole chain projecting to one side of the post. The shoulder, elbow, and wrist are serial revolute joints carried by successive members.

A segmented finger-like phalanx chain. A grounded root knuckle carries proximal, middle, and distal phalanx links with a blunt fingertip pad and visible side cheeks at every joint. Three revolute joints connect the phalanx links in one bending plane.

A boxed folding arm chain. A grounded base plate carries three flat bar links in series and a small end tab, with the inner links intentionally deeper than the outer one. The arm uses three serial revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane.

A ladder-frame four-link hinge chain. A fixed base lug supports five bar-like members joined by evenly spaced hinge blocks, ending in a small output plate. The mechanism is a four-joint serial revolute chain in one plane.

A double-leaf driveway-style garden gate with two framed leaves that meet at the center. Each leaf has a rectangular perimeter frame, sparse horizontal rails, and its own hinge set mounted to a side post. Each leaf rotates on a vertical revolute hinge axis with about 120 degrees of travel so the pair can open outward.

A pair of round wire-rim glasses with slightly curved temple arms and small nose-pad brackets. The front frame is a rigid bridge and rim assembly, and each temple is attached at the outer rim by a knuckle hinge. Each temple arm rotates on its own revolute hinge axis so the pair can fold flat.

An automotive glove compartment door integrated into a simple dashboard opening. A shallow bin sits behind the opening, and a rectangular front door with a handle is hinged along its lower edge to the housing. One revolute joint along the bottom edge lets the door swing downward about 90 degrees.

A small wall cabinet with two side-hinged doors. A rectangular box body has a top, bottom, two sides, and a central shelf, with each front door mounted to an outer side panel. Each door rotates on a vertical revolute hinge axis about 110 degrees.

A rectangular hard equipment case for a compact instrument. The base shell has rounded corners and a flat front face with latches, and the top shell is hinged at the back. One rear revolute joint lets the lid open about 100 degrees.
A boxed lever chain. A grounded base lug supports three rigid lever bars in series and a small tab at the free end. All three joints are revolute joints about parallel supported axes in one plane.

A pedestal-mounted missile launcher with a boxy launch pod. A fixed base supports a yaw turntable, which carries a tilting cradle with the rectangular canister pack mounted on top. The launcher yaws about a vertical revolute axis at the base and pitches on a horizontal revolute axis at the cradle sides.

A multi-segment fold-out arm. A fixed base plate carries four short boxed links in series ending at a small platform bracket. The four revolute joints fold in one plane so the arm packs near the base and extends outward into a straight or slightly arched reach.

An overshot waterwheel on a simple support frame. A large spoked wheel with a ring of shallow buckets is mounted between two side supports, and a short chute sits above the rim near the top. The wheel rotates continuously about its horizontal axle revolute axis between the supports.

A bridge-backed pitch-roll module. A rear support frame carries a compact outer ring and a smaller inner cradle that stays clearly nested inside it. The outer and inner members are revolute joints on perpendicular supported axes.

A French-door refrigerator with two upper doors and a lower freezer drawer. The upper cabinet has paired hinged doors meeting at the center, and the freezer compartment below uses a wide pull-out drawer on rails. The two upper doors rotate on vertical revolute hinge axes at the cabinet sides, and the lower freezer drawer slides prismatically on telescoping side rails.

A box cutter style retractable utility knife. A tapered handle shell contains a straight blade slider that emerges from the front nose, with a thumb button riding in a top slot. The blade carrier is a prismatic slider along the handle centerline with about 40 mm of travel.

A swing-arm extension chain. A grounded support carries a single arm link, and the arm link carries a compact telescoping nose slide. The arm rotates on a revolute joint at the root, and the nose slide is a prismatic joint.

A shoulder-elbow-extension chain. A grounded base bracket supports two hinged links, and the distal link supports a short slider at its tip. The shoulder and elbow are revolute joints, and the tip stage is a prismatic joint.
A fork-root three-joint arm. A grounded fork captures the first arm link, followed by a forearm and a short wrist flange. The arm has shoulder, elbow, and wrist revolute joints in sequence.

An undershot waterwheel. Two side frames support a horizontal axle carrying a paddle wheel below a simple channel or trough edge. Joints: the wheel rotates continuously about the axle axis. Use one central support under the rotating stage.

A upright lift slide with wrist plate. A fixed mast supports a moving carriage, and the carriage supports a short hinged plate with a plain front face. The carriage moves on a vertical prismatic joint, and the plate rotates on a supported revolute hinge.

A wheelbarrow. A tray sits between two handles, with one front wheel in a fork and two resting legs at the rear. Joints: the front wheel rotates continuously about its axle. Push the wheels farther outboard for a wider stance.

category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: A pedestal three-branch tooling tree. A round pedestal carries three side-supported rotary branches with different reach lengths and compact rectangular end faces. The branches are independent revolute joints about separate supported axes on the pedestal.
A residential front-load dryer with a white rectangular body, a drum rotating on a central revolute axle, and a circular porthole door with a glass window hinged on the left edge.

A commercial front-load washing machine with a wide rectangular body, a large porthole door, and a pull-out detergent drawer. The front door opens on revolute hinges at the door's vertical left edge. The detergent drawer slides prismatically on horizontal guide rails.

A stacked reduction gear bench assembly. A grounded side frame supports four parallel shafts carrying large and small spur gears in a stepped reduction layout. The shafts are independent revolute joints about their supported axes.

A standard upright hand truck. A tubular frame rises from a flat toe plate, with two large wheels mounted on an axle at the lower rear corners and loop handles at the top. Each wheel spins continuously on the axle, while the frame itself stays rigid.

A carriage-shoulder-tip slide chain. A fixed linear guide carries a moving carriage, the carriage supports a hinged arm, and the arm carries a short end slide. The joints are prismatic at the base, revolute at the arm root, and prismatic at the tip slide.

A slide-shoulder-elbow chain. A grounded base slide carries a moving carriage, the carriage carries a shoulder link, and the shoulder link carries a forelink. The root is a prismatic joint followed by shoulder and elbow revolute joints.

A swing-slide-output chain. A grounded bracket supports a hinged primary link, the primary link carries a compact runner, and the runner supports a short rotating tip face. The joints are revolute, then prismatic, then revolute.

A rotary table with tilting trunnion. A grounded base turntable supports two side cheeks, and a square or circular work table sits between the cheeks on a transverse shaft. The base rotates on a vertical revolute joint, and the work table tilts on a horizontal revolute joint carried by the side cheeks.

A side-hinge TV wall mount shown without the screen. A grounded wall plate supports two arm links and a compact display head with distinct tilt and swivel support pieces. The arm uses two folding revolute joints, and the head adds one swivel revolute and one tilt revolute.

A vacuum cleaner with an articulated wand. A main body connects to a rigid wand chain ending in a floor nozzle. Joints: the wand bends on one or two revolute elbows, and the floor nozzle pitches on a horizontal revolute hinge. Offset the moving chain to one side of the fixed support.

A manual wheelchair. A chair frame supports two large rear wheels, two front casters, a seat, a backrest, and footrests. Joints: each rear wheel rotates continuously about its axle, and each front caster swivels about a vertical stem and spins continuously about its wheel axle. Push the wheels farther outboard for a wider stance.

An industrial air pump with a tall cylinder, a padded T-handle, and a telescoping lower stabilizer bar across the base, plus a folding handle grip around the top bar. the stabilizer stays clearly nested in the lower frame, and the grip remains a supported sleeve on the crossbar. The T-handle and piston rod move prismatically along the cylinder axis and the lower stabilizer bar slides prismatically across its guide, and the grip rotates continuously about the crossbar axis. Industrial floor-pump scale.

A dual-actuation dropper seatpost with a round outer tube, a telescoping inner tube, and two side-lever actuators. The inner tube slides prismatically inside the outer tube. Each side lever pivots on its own revolute hinge at the collar clamp ring.

A palm-sized camcorder with a rounded handgrip, a short front lens barrel, and a flip-out side monitor attached near the rear half of the body. The side monitor rotates outward on a vertical hinge line, the lens ring rotates continuously on the barrel, and a hinged port cover rotates on a bottom-edge hinge along the side wall under the screen mount. Compact handheld device scale.

A triple-dial candy vending machine with three stacked bins in a tall body, separate rotary selectors, and one lower retrieval door. The three selector dials rotate continuously on parallel horizontal shafts, and the retrieval door rotates downward on a bottom horizontal hinge.

A slant-top casino machine with an angled screen panel, a button deck, and a lockable belly door beneath the play area. The belly door rotates on a vertical side revolute hinge, a spin button on the deck translates prismatically in its sleeve, and a volume knob near the screen rotates continuously about its shaft.

A laboratory sample chest freezer with an insulated rectangular body and a heavy hinged lid with a pressure seal. The lid opens on two revolute hinge knuckles at the lid's rear edge. A lid locking latch on the front edge operates on a revolute cam latch joint.

category_title: Dishwasher with dropdown door and sliding racks
prompt: A contemporary built-in dishwasher with a concealed top strip, two sliding racks, and twin fold-down mug shelves integrated into the upper rack. The dropdown door rotates on a lower horizontal hinge, both racks slide prismatically on their side rails, and the two spray arms rotate continuously on central hubs in the tub. The mug shelves rotate on matched side pivots, the start button and three option buttons on the top strip are separate small prismatic push buttons, and the rinse-aid cap on the door liner rotates continuously. The tub and door interior should remain thin-walled and hollow. Standard kitchen scale.

category_title: Display freezer with sliding glass lids
prompt: A low-profile display freezer with a narrow sliding service hatch in one lid. The broad cabinet has two large top glass panels, and one panel carries a smaller hatch set into it for quick access. The two main lids slide prismatically on the top frame, and the small service hatch rotates on a short hinge within one lid panel.

A dock loading ramp with a wide steel deck, a hinged lip at the front edge, and two folding support leg pairs mounted under the deck. The lip and leg pairs remain visibly supported by real hinge brackets beneath the deck structure. The main deck rotates on a rear horizontal storage hinge, the front lip rotates on a horizontal hinge at the deck edge, and the support leg pairs rotate on their underside hinge pins. Loading-bay ramp scale.

An A3 wide-format flatbed scanner with a wide rectangular body and a lid hinged at the lid's rear edge. The lid opens on two revolute hinge knuckles at the rear. An internal scan head moves prismatically along the scan axis inside the body.

A floor-standing drafting table with a trestle base, an upper tilt frame, and a large rectangular drawing surface. The drawing surface rotates on horizontal side pivots carried by the tilt frame, the height carriage slides prismatically up and down on the central upright, and a locking knob at one side rotates on its short shaft.

A prep food processor with a clear bowl, a twist-lock base coupling, a tall pusher in the feed tube, and a lower front panel carrying a timer dial beside a side power rocker. The bowl rotates on a short continuous locking joint, the pusher slides prismatically in the tube, and the grating basket rotates continuously about the center axis. The timer dial rotates continuously, and the side power rocker pivots independently on a short hinge. The bowl and chute should remain hollow and open above the spinning basket. Countertop kitchen scale.

A classroom globe on a pedestal stand with a full circular meridian. The sphere sits between top and bottom trunnions inside the ring, and the ring is held by a forked support from the base. The sphere rotates about its north-south revolute axis, and the meridian tilts on a horizontal revolute axis at the fork.

A top-hinged awning window in a rectangular frame. A glazed sash sits inside the outer frame and is supported from the top edge by two compact hinges. The sash rotates outward on a horizontal upper revolute axis by about 60 degrees.

A turbofan jet engine nacelle on a short display stand. A cylindrical nacelle encloses a central spinner, a front fan stage, and a rear exhaust cone aligned on the same centerline. The fan rotor spins continuously about the main longitudinal revolute axis inside the intake.

A folding kick scooter with a narrow deck and upright handlebar stem. Two small wheels support the deck front and rear, and the stem rises from a front fork with a hinge block near the deck nose. Both wheels spin continuously on their axles, and the stem folds on a revolute hinge near the deck through about 170 degrees.

A medical knee scooter with a padded knee platform and bicycle-style handlebars. A lower frame carries four small wheels, with the front pair in a steerable fork assembly and the rear pair on fixed supports under the frame. The four wheels rotate on their axles, and the front steering assembly yaws on a vertical revolute axis at the head tube.

category_title: Laptop stand with articulated height adjustment
prompt: A low-profile laptop stand with a base board, a rigid platform hinged along the rear edge of the base, and an articulated rear prop arm under the platform. The prop arm includes a short telescoping section for height adjustment. The platform rotates on a rear horizontal hinge, the prop arm rotates on a lower horizontal base pivot and an upper horizontal platform pivot, and the telescoping arm stage slides prismatically along its length.

A traditional pyramid-cased metronome on a flat stepped base. A slender pendulum rod rises through the apex slot carrying a disc-shaped tempo weight; a cylindrical winding key protrudes from the rear lower face. The pendulum swings on a revolute joint at the apex pivot shaft; the weight rides prismatically along the rod; the key turns continuously on its shaft.

A bench-post monitor mount without the display. A fixed post base supports a lower link, an outer link, and a rectangular head frame with distinct pan and tilt supports. The lower link, outer link, head swivel, and head tilt are four revolute joints.

A ferris-wheel style observation wheel on an A-frame support. A large circular rim with spoke members is carried by side towers, and small gondolas hang from pivots around the rim. The wheel rotates on a central horizontal revolute axis, and each gondola pivots on its own hanger revolute axis so it can remain upright.

A mesh-backed office chair with a five-wheel base and a simple tilt mechanism. The seat sits above a central pedestal, the back frame rises from the rear bracket, and paired armrests flank the seat. The caster wheels rotate continuously on their axles, and the seat-back assembly tilts about a transverse revolute axis through roughly 15 degrees.

A conductor-style orchestra music stand with a broad desk, a single pedestal mast, and folding page supports at the left and right edges. The desk tilts on a horizontal hinge at the mast head, the mast extends prismatically from the pedestal sleeve, and the two page supports rotate on small side pivots.

category_title: Pedal trash bin with hinged lid
prompt: A kitchen pedal trash bin with a tall rectangular body and a rear-hinged lid. The rigid shell has a wide foot pedal projecting from the lower front face and a simple lid spanning the top opening. The lid rotates upward on a horizontal hinge at the back rim, and the pedal rotates on a transverse pivot near the base of the body.

A compact retractable bollard with a short round post, a ground sleeve with drain slots, and a lock housing built into the top face. The post slides prismatically inside the sleeve, a hinged lock cover rotates on a tiny side hinge at the top face, and the lock core rotates continuously on its center axis. Small parking-control hardware scale.

A modern rice cooker with an oval housing, a top-opening lid, and a small steam vent cap near the rear of the lid. The lid opens on a rear revolute hinge, and the vent cap rotates on a short local hinge at the vent opening. Countertop scale with a low, wide body.

A fold-out drying rack with side wings. A central rack frame carries two wing frames and a lower support frame with hanging rails across the spans. Joints: each wing frame is revolute about its outer hinge line, and the lower support frame folds on its own revolute hinges. Keep the folded envelope narrow and easy to store.

category_title: Sluice gate with vertical lift panel
prompt: A masonry-mounted sluice gate with steel guide jambs, a flat vertical lift panel, and a compact gearbox perched on the lintel beam. The lift panel moves prismatically along the jamb guides, a crank wheel rotates continuously on the gearbox input shaft, and the gearbox front cover rotates on a side hinge for servicing. Water-channel control scale.

category_title: Top-load washing machine with hinged lid
prompt: A residential top-load washing machine with a boxy cabinet, a large top lid, a deep cylindrical wash tub, and a rear console with a cycle dial plus four separate buttons. The lid rotates upward on a rear horizontal revolute hinge, the inner wash tub rotates continuously on the vertical center axis inside the cabinet opening, the cycle dial rotates continuously on the rear console, and all four visible console buttons are independent small prismatic push buttons. A lid-lock plunger at the front edge moves prismatically into the top deck when closed. The tub opening should read as a real hollow laundry cavity with depth below the top deck rather than a shallow solid recess. Full laundry-appliance scale.

A narrow tower cooler fan with a softly rounded column, a low round pedestal, and a pair of rotary controls inset into a beveled top cap. The vertical blower rotor spins continuously inside the housing, the whole column oscillates on a revolute joint over the pedestal, and each control rotates on its own small shaft. Household product scale with believable plastic wall thickness.

A toy coupe car with a simple two-door body, a short hood, a rear trunk volume, and four exposed wheels in open arches. The four wheels rotate continuously on their axle shafts, and the two side doors rotate outward on vertical hinges at the A-pillars.
A deep-link two-joint arm chain. A fixed root bracket supports three rigid links that step down in depth toward the tip. The mechanism uses two serial revolute joints about parallel supported axes.

category_title: Wall Safe With Hinged Door And Dial
prompt: A recessed wall safe with a broad trim flange, a heavy left-hinged door, a large round dial, and a multi-spoke wheel handle. The door rotates on a vertical revolute hinge, the dial rotates continuously about its center, and the wheel handle rotates on its coaxial shaft at the door center.

A wheelie bin with a top lid. A bin body sits on a rear axle with two wheels and carries one full-width lid along the back edge. Joints: the lid is revolute about a horizontal rear hinge axis, and the rear wheels spin continuously about the axle. Keep the body shallow and compact around the opening.

A horizontal-axis wind turbine. A tower supports a nacelle and a front hub with multiple blades. Joints: the rotor spins continuously about the main shaft axis, and the nacelle yaws about the vertical tower axis. Use one central support under the rotating stage.

A car windshield sun visor. A padded visor panel hangs from a roof bracket with a main pivot arm and a secondary side-swing joint. Joints: the visor flips down on a horizontal revolute hinge and swings sideways on a vertical revolute pivot. Keep the body shallow and compact around the opening.

A windshield wiper assembly with one arm and one blade. A motor housing supports a wiper spindle, a sweep arm, and a blade carrier at the tip. Joints: the arm sweeps on a revolute joint about the spindle axis, and the blade carrier rolls on a second revolute joint along the arm. Offset the moving chain to one side of the fixed support.

A brushed-metal zippo-style lighter with a boxy lower shell, an upper lid, and a visible chimney insert inside the case, plus a small cam lever inside the lid edge. the insert remains a separate supported part seated inside the outer shell, and the cam lever remains a separate supported piece beside the hinge. The lid opens on a narrow side hinge and the spark wheel rotates continuously above the insert body, and the cam lever rotates on its short local pivot when the lid opens. Pocket-accessory scale.

A boxed gimbal joystick. A square base housing supports an outer pitch yoke, an inner roll cradle, and a short central lever capped by a simple block. The mechanism uses two orthogonal revolute joints at the gimbal supports.
